text.title: Antipatterns and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Common failure modes in LLM-generated Rails service code: divergent ServiceResult, non-idempotent jobs, N+1, and authorization gaps.

antipattern.description: Multiple files defining ServiceResult with different method names (ok?, success?, result?, error?).
antipattern.reason: Controllers must handle every variant. Add a CI step that greps for class ServiceResult and fails if more than one definition exists. Lock to success?/failure? only.

antipattern.description: Sidekiq jobs that accept ActiveRecord objects directly: perform(user) instead of perform(user.id).
antipattern.reason: ActiveRecord objects serialise as YAML and load a potentially stale record when the worker runs. Pass IDs; load fresh records inside perform.

antipattern.description: Sidekiq jobs without idempotency — double-charging, double-sending on retry.
antipattern.reason: Workers retry on failure. Idempotency requires checking a unique key before performing the side effect. Use a database flag or SidekiqUniqueJobs.

antipattern.description: N+1 queries in ActiveModel::Serializer or Jbuilder templates, not caught by controller specs.
antipattern.reason: Serialisers run after the controller action. Bullet must be enabled in test mode with raise: true and serialiser specs must be run, not just controller specs.

rule.statement: ApplicationController must call verify_authorized and verify_policy_scoped (Pundit). Every controller action must call authorize @resource or policy_scope(Resource) explicitly.
rule.rationale: Pundit does not enforce authorization automatically. Without verify_authorized, an agent adding a new action may forget to call authorize, leaving it unprotected.

rule.statement: Never generate drop_column or change_column migrations without a backfill plan. Require reversible migrations — every change_table block must have a reversible counterpart.
rule.rationale: Irreversible migrations cannot be rolled back. Agents generate destructive migrations routinely without checking if the column has data or if the rollback is needed during a deploy.

text.reference: RSpec configuration enabling Bullet N+1 detection with raise: true
